summary: Matched the common fighter neutral escape action callbacks by replacing four assembly stubs in `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` with C implementations in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Escape.c`. The matched callbacks are `ftCo_EscapeN_Anim`, `ftCo_EscapeN_IASA`, `ftCo_EscapeN_Phys`, and `ftCo_EscapeN_Coll`, bringing the reported `ftCo_Escape.c` file score to 100%.
what_changed: Removed the assembly definitions for `ftCo_EscapeN_Anim`, `ftCo_EscapeN_IASA`, `ftCo_EscapeN_Phys`, and `ftCo_EscapeN_Coll` from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`.
Added C implementations of the same four neutral escape callbacks to `ftCo_Escape.c`.
Changed helper function parameter types for `ftCo_800998EC` and `ftCo_80099954` from `Fighter_GObj*` to `ftCo_GObj*`, matching surrounding common-fighter conventions and the new callback call sites.
Kept the remaining local assembly label `.L_80099A58` in `ft_08A4.s` and made it global after the removed functions, indicating only this slice of the asm file was decompiled.
`ftCo_EscapeN_Anim` now directly expresses the assembly behavior: call `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj)`, and if no frames remain, call `ft_8008A2BC(gobj)`.
`ftCo_EscapeN_IASA` was matched as an empty callback.
`ftCo_EscapeN_Phys` was matched as a simple wrapper around `ft_80084F3C(gobj)`.
`ftCo_EscapeN_Coll` was matched as a simple wrapper around `ft_80084104(gobj)`.
smart_moves: Recognized that three of the callbacks were trivial wrappers or no-ops, avoiding overcomplicated C and matching the tiny assembly bodies directly.
Used `if (!ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj))` in `ftCo_EscapeN_Anim`, which cleanly corresponds to the assembly `cmpwi`/`bne` branch around `ft_8008A2BC`.
Adjusted nearby function signatures to `ftCo_GObj*`, improving local type consistency for common fighter code without changing behavior shown in the diff.
Removed only the now-matched assembly while preserving the later unresolved `.L_80099A58` assembly block, keeping the boundary between matched and unmatched code explicit.
decomp_lessons: For action callback sets, match the four standard callbacks independently: animation, IASA, physics, and collision may be simple no-op/wrapper functions.
An IASA callback that is only `blr` in assembly can be represented as an empty C function with the correct signature.
Wrapper functions around known engine helpers often match cleanly as single-call C functions when the argument is passed through unchanged.

When decompiling a contiguous assembly region, it is acceptable to remove only the matched functions and leave later unresolved labels in assembly, but labels may need visibility adjustments such as `.global .L_80099A58`.
Type aliases like `ftCo_GObj*` can matter for consistency and future matching even when ABI behavior is unchanged from `Fighter_GObj*`.
naming_conventions: Neutral escape callbacks use the `ftCo_EscapeN_*` prefix with suffixes `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, and `Coll`.
Common fighter object parameters in this file are represented as `ftCo_GObj* gobj`.
Common fighter state helpers in this area use the `ftCo_80099xxx` address-derived naming style when not yet semantically named.
Motion state constants follow the `ftCo_MS_*` form, as shown by `ftCo_MS_EscapeN`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted an assembly conditional branch into a direct negated condition: `if (!ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj)) { ft_8008A2BC(gobj); }`.
Matched a bare `blr` callback as `void ftCo_EscapeN_IASA(ftCo_GObj* gobj) {}`.
Matched stack-frame wrapper assembly around a single branch-and-link call as simple C passthrough wrappers for physics and collision callbacks.
Deleted 45 lines of assembly after adding 21 lines of C, indicating a net reduction through idiomatic trivial C implementations.
The PR report states `ftCo_Escape.c` reached `100.00%`, with `ftCo_EscapeN_Anim`, `ftCo_EscapeN_IASA`, `ftCo_EscapeN_Phys`, and `ftCo_EscapeN_Coll` each at `100.00%`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `ft_8008A2BC`, `ft_80084F3C`, or `ft_80084104` to semantic helper names.
Check whether the remaining `.L_80099A58` block in `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` was decompiled in a later PR.
Search for other `EscapeN` or escape callback implementations to confirm shared callback patterns across common fighter states.
Look for subsequent type cleanup replacing `Fighter_GObj*` with `ftCo_GObj*` in nearby ftCommon files.
